clinical_history: This ECG from a 68-year-old woman is diagnostic of which ONE of the following?:
diagnosis: b)  Note loss of R waves V1 - V4, with ST elevations in  V2 - V5/V6 (as well as slightly in I and aVL) with underlying sinus rhythm.  Marked left axis deviation is present with a normal QRS duration consistent with left anterior fascicular block (LAFB). (However, by itself, LAFB does NOT account for the loss of R wave progression.)  The patient had had a very recent ST elevation/Q wave anterior myocardial infarction (MI).  Cardiac catheterization revealed 3-vessel disease with a 90~ mid-LAD "culprit" lesion that was treated successfully with angioplasty/stenting.
